title: messages: Profile.fields has no size bound; hostile peer causes unbounded heap allocation
description: ClearMessage::Profile { fields: HashMap<String, String> } has no bound on map size or key/value length enforced at deserialization. A hostile MLS peer can send a Profile message with thousands of entries each with arbitrarily large strings. Since Profile is deserialized from MLS-decrypted bytes, the allocation happens before any application-level check. Add a custom deserializer that caps total field count (e.g. 32) and key/value length (e.g. 256 bytes each), analogous to the existing FileChunk/FileHeader validators.
